ogTitle: St Arnaud vs St Arnaud North: Climate Safety

heading: St Arnaud vs St Arnaud North — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between St Arnaud, Victoria and St Arnaud North, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: St Arnaud North has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to St Arnaud (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is St Arnaud or St Arnaud North safer from flooding?
faq.answer: St Arnaud has a flood risk score of 50/100, while St Arnaud North scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: St Arnaud scores 32/100 for bushfire risk. St Arnaud North scores 37/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in St Arnaud or St Arnaud North?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. St Arnaud North has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
